name: Pnar

ancestors.notes: Descent per Glottolog classification of Pnar (pnar1238).

notes: Austroasiatic (Khasian) language of Meghalaya, India, written in the missionary-derived Roman orthography shared with Khasi. The map applies the Khasi letter values (aspirate and breathy digraphs ph/bh/th/dh/kh/jh, ng for the velar nasal, ñ for the palatal nasal, ï for the high central vowel, acute accent for vowel length), since Pnar spelling in practice follows the Khasi system; a proposed distinct Jaintia alphabet never gained traction and Pnar spelling remains unstandardized. Pnar is atonal. Left unencoded: vowel length where the acute is omitted (common in Pnar writing), the syllable-final reading of h as a glottal stop, and dialectal e/ɛ alternations, all of which raise the expected error floor.
